region_of_origin: South Asia / Central Asia (widely cultivated globally)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of honeydew melon is approximately 150g, equivalent to one medium wedge or one cup of diced fruit, commonly consumed as a snack or dessert component.

nutrient_source.title: Honeydew Melon, raw — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 09184)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for raw honeydew melon used as the primary nutrient reference. The input HPB record contained several anomalous values (protein 0g, carbohydrate 0.06g, sugar 0g, calcium 228mg, iron 11mg, cholesterol 36mg) that are inconsistent with all known approved sources for fresh honeydew melon. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy values have been adopted as the authoritative reference. Singapore HPB database was also consulted but the input values appear to reflect a data entry error in the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Honeydew melon GI of 65 (medium) is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables published in Diabetes Care. Glycemic load per 150g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 13.6) / 100 = 8.84, rounded to 8.8. The input record cited GI 58 from an 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved source; the University of Sydney GI Database value of 65 is used instead.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (18mg/100g) and high potassium (228mg/100g) supporting DASH diet principles
hypertension.guidance: Honeydew melon is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its low sodium content (18mg/100g) and high potassium (228mg/100g), which supports vasodilation and blood pressure regulation consistent with DASH dietary guidelines; a 150g serving provides approximately 342mg potassium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI of 65 and natural sugar content of 8.12g/100g requiring portion control
type2_diabetes.guidance: Honeydew melon has a moderate glycemic index of 65 and contains approximately 8.12g natural sugar per 100g, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 8.8 per 150g serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portions to 100–150g and avoid honeydew juice or syrup-preserved forms to manage postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.04g/100g), zero cholesterol, and potassium-rich profile supporting cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Honeydew melon is cardioprotective due to its negligible saturated fat (0.04g/100g), zero cholesterol, and high potassium content (228mg/100g); regular consumption as part of a fruit-rich diet supports endothelial function and is consistent with AHA dietary recommendations for cardiovascular disease prevention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honeydew melon (Cucumis melo var. inodorus) is a large, round to oval fresh fruit with a smooth, pale green to cream-coloured rind and sweet, juicy, pale green flesh. It is consumed raw as a snack, dessert fruit, or ingredient in fruit salads, smoothies, and cold beverages. The texture is firm yet succulent, with a mild, floral sweetness and low acidity. Honeydew is widely available in Singapore, Southeast Asia, and globally, and is commonly sold in wedges, cubed, or as juice. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 36 kcal, 9.1g carbohydrate, 8.1g natural sugar, 0.54g protein, 0.14g fat, 0.8g dietary fibre, 18mg sodium, and 228mg potassium. It is an excellent source of vitamin C (18mg/100g) and folate (19mcg/100g). Its moderate glycemic index of 65 means portion control is advisable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The high potassium and low sodium profile makes it suitable for hypertension management under DASH dietary principles. Honeydew is naturally cholesterol-free, low in saturated fat, and appropriate for vegan, vegetarian, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. In Singapore and Chinese communities, it is known as 哈密瓜 (Ha Mi Gua) and is a popular fruit for gifting and festive occasions.

honeydew melon: winter melon (colloquial, not to be confused with wax gourd)

All mandatory fields are complete.
notes: CRITICAL DATA CORRECTION: The input HPB record contained multiple anomalous per_100g values that are inconsistent with all approved sources for fresh honeydew melon: protein 0g (USDA: 0.54g), carbohydrate 0.06g (USDA: 9.09g), sugar 0g (USDA: 8.12g), calcium 228mg (USDA: 6mg), iron 11mg (USDA: 0.17mg), cholesterol 36mg (USDA: 0mg), energy 90kcal (USDA: 36kcal). These values have been replaced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data (FDC ID 09184). The HPB input data appears to reflect a data entry error or record mismatch. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central — honeydew melon contains no cholesterol as it is a plant-based food (naturally zero). vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero as honeydew is a plant food containing no B12. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. monounsaturated_fat_g set to 0.0 per USDA data. trans_fat_g is naturally zero for raw fresh fruit. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g reported as 0.0 per USDA (not -1). All other -1 fields in the template have been populated with USDA data where available. GI SOURCE NOTE: Input GI of 58 from 'Embedded GI Database' was not from an approved source; replaced with GI 65 from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. KETO TAG REMOVED: The input dietary_tags included 'keto' which is incorrect for honeydew melon given its 9.09g carbohydrate per 100g content; this tag has been removed. RA HEALTH CONDITION: The input included a rheumatoid_arthritis health suitability entry; this has been omitted from health_context as the three mandatory conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) are required and rheumatoid arthritis is not a mandatory condition under the scoring model.
